VENDOR COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST
===========================
Metro Retail Group — Vendor Onboarding Requirements
Version: 3.2 | Effective: January 2026

All new vendors must satisfy the following 12 requirements before
being approved to supply products to Metro Retail Group.

 1. BUSINESS REGISTRATION
    - Valid business license or certificate of incorporation
    - Federal Tax ID (EIN) on file
    - State resale certificate (if applicable)

 2. INSURANCE COVERAGE
    - General liability: minimum $2,000,000 per occurrence
    - Product liability: minimum $1,000,000 per occurrence
    - Workers compensation: as required by state law
    - Certificate of Insurance naming Metro Retail Group as additional insured

 3. PRODUCT SAFETY & COMPLIANCE
    - All products must comply with applicable CPSC regulations
    - MSDS/SDS sheets required for chemical products
    - California Prop 65 labeling where applicable
    - CE marking for electronics sold in applicable markets

 4. QUALITY ASSURANCE
    - ISO 9001 certification (preferred) or equivalent QA documentation
    - Incoming quality inspection pass rate >= 98%
    - Defect rate must not exceed 2% per shipment

 5. LABELING & PACKAGING
    - UPC/EAN barcodes on all individual units
    - Master carton labeling per Metro Retail spec (GS1-128)
    - Country of origin clearly marked
    - Lot/batch number traceable on all units

 6. EDI CAPABILITY
    - EDI 850 (Purchase Order) receive capability
    - EDI 856 (ASN) send capability
    - EDI 810 (Invoice) send capability
    - Trading partner setup within 30 days of approval

 7. SHIPPING & LOGISTICS
    - On-time delivery rate >= 95%
    - ASN transmitted within 24 hours of shipment
    - Pallet configuration per Metro Retail DC specifications
    - Appointment scheduling via vendor portal

 8. FINANCIAL STABILITY
    - Dun & Bradstreet report (DUNS number required)
    - Minimum 2 years in business
    - Two trade references required
    - Bank reference letter

 9. SUSTAINABILITY & ETHICS
    - No child labor or forced labor in supply chain
    - Conflict minerals disclosure (if applicable)
    - Environmental compliance documentation
    - Code of conduct acknowledgment signed

10. DATA & PRIVACY
    - PII handling procedures documented
    - Data breach notification commitment (72 hours)
    - CCPA/GDPR compliance where applicable

11. SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT
    - Order fulfillment within 5 business days
    - Fill rate >= 96%
    - Chargeback dispute resolution within 15 business days
    - Dedicated account representative assigned

12. RETURN & DEFECT POLICY
    - Accept returns for defective merchandise (no restocking fee)
    - Credit memo issued within 10 business days
    - Recall procedures documented and tested annually
    - Product replacement turnaround <= 10 business days
